communicative_purpose: To indicate a museum or attraction is not open
trigger_situations: Visiting museums, opening hours, travel plans
variations: fermé pour rénovation, fermé le mardi, pas ouvert
common_mistakes: Many French museums close on Mondays or Tuesdays, not always Sunday
collocates: aujourd'hui, pour travaux, le lundi, cause Covid
example_1: Le musée du Louvre est fermé le mardi.
example_2: Le musée est fermé pour rénovation jusqu'en mars.
example_3: On est venus et le musée est fermé, quelle déception !
example_1_translation: The Louvre Museum is closed on Tuesdays.
example_2_translation: The museum is closed for renovations until March.
example_3_translation: We came and the museum is closed, what a disappointment!

communicative_purpose: To refer to when a venue is open to visitors
variations: les heures d'ouverture, les heures d'ouverture, quand ouvre
common_mistakes: Confusing with closing hours; ask for complete schedule
example_1: Quels sont les horaires d'ouverture du musée ?
example_2: Les horaires d'ouverture sont de 9h à 18h en été.
example_3: On peut consulter les horaires d'ouverture sur le site internet.
example_1_translation: What are the museum opening hours?
example_2_translation: Opening hours are 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. in summer.
example_3_translation: You can consult the opening hours on the website.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a ticket for entering a venue
variations: le ticket, l'entrée, le pass, la billet
common_mistakes: 'Billet' can mean any ticket; 'entrée' is the act of entering
example_1: Le billet d'entrée pour le château est 12 euros.
example_2: Les enfants ont le billet d'entrée gratuit jusqu'à 12 ans.
example_3: On peut acheter le billet d'entrée en ligne pour éviter la queue.
example_1_translation: The entrance ticket to the castle is 12 euros.
example_3_translation: You can buy the entry ticket online to avoid the queue.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a tour led by a guide
variations: le tour guidé, la visite avec guide, le parcours guidé
common_mistakes: Confusing with self-guided visits; guided tours have extra cost
example_1: La visite guidée du château dure environ 2 heures.
example_2: On a pris une visite guidée en anglais pour le palais.
example_3: La visite guidée coûte 8 euros de plus que l'entrée simple.
example_1_translation: The guided tour of the castle lasts approximately 2 hours.
example_2_translation: We took a guided tour in English for the palace.
example_3_translation: The guided tour costs 8 euros more than single entry.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the local tourism information center
variations: le bureau d'information, le centre touristique, l tourist info
common_mistakes: English terms rarely used; French 'office de tourisme' is standard
example_1: On peut prendre un plan à l'office de tourisme.
example_2: L'office de tourisme est en face de la gare.
example_3: L'office de tourisme propose des visites gratuites du vieux quartier.
example_1_translation: You can get a map at the tourist office.
example_2_translation: The tourist office is opposite the station.
example_3_translation: The tourist office offers free tours of the old quarter.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a protected historical building or site
trigger_situations: Heritage sites, cultural tourism, historical visits
variations: le patrimoine, le site historique, le monument classé
common_mistakes: Historic monuments in France are often free entry on first Sunday
example_1: Ce château est un monument historique depuis 1880.
example_2: Le Mont Saint-Michel est un monument historique famous worldwide.
example_3: Les monuments historiques sont souvent protégés par l'État.
example_1_translation: This castle has been a historic monument since 1880.
example_2_translation: Mont Saint-Michel is a famous worldwide historical monument.
example_3_translation: Historical monuments are often protected by the state.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a line of people waiting
variations: la queue, la file, faire la queue, attendre dans la file
common_mistakes: 'File' is queue; 'queue' also means tail; both used
example_1: Il y a une longue file d'attente pour le Musée d'Orsay.
example_2: On a fait la queue pendant une heure pour entrer.
example_3: Avec le Paris Pass, on peut éviter la file d'attente.
example_1_translation: There is a long queue for the Musée d'Orsay.
example_2_translation: We waited in line for an hour to get in.
example_3_translation: With the Paris Pass, you can avoid the queue.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a wide, scenic view from a location
variations: la vue, le point de vue, la perspective, le paysage
common_mistakes: 'Panorama' implies 180+ degree view; 'vue' is general view
collocates: magnifique, vue, 360 degrés, sur la ville
example_1: Du haut de la Tour Eiffel, le panorama sur Paris est incroyable.
example_2: Il y a un panorama beautiful depuis ce belvédère.
example_3: Le panorama à 360 degrés permet de voir toute la ville.
example_1_translation: From the top of the Eiffel Tower, the panorama of Paris is incredible.
example_2_translation: There is a beautiful panorama from this lookout.
example_3_translation: The 360-degree panorama allows you to see the entire city.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a specific statue
variations: la statue, le statue, le monument, la sculpture
example_2: La statue de la Liberté est à New York, pas à Paris.
example_3: On a pris une photo devant la statue de Napoléon.
example_1_translation: This statue represents Joan of Arc.
example_2_translation: The Statue of Liberty is in New York, not Paris.
example_3_translation: We took a photo in front of the statue of Napoleon.

communicative_purpose: To express that something is worth doing or seeing
variations: je recommande, à voir, à ne pas manquer, vaut le détour
common_mistakes: 'Recommandé' vs 'recommandable' - both exist with different nuances
example_1: Le quartier du Marais, c'est vraiment recommandé pour un week-end.
example_2: Je recommande ce restaurant, la cuisine est excellente.
example_3: C'est recommandé de réserver à l'avance pour les sites populaires.
example_1_translation: The Marais district is really recommended for a weekend.
example_2_translation: I recommend this restaurant, the food is excellent.
example_3_translation: Advance reservations are recommended for popular sites.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a pass offering access to multiple attractions
variations: le Paris Pass, la carte touristique, le City Pass, le pass
common_mistakes: Various city passes exist; check what's included before buying
example_1: Le Paris Pass inclut l'entrée à plus de 60 musées.
example_2: J'ai pris le pass culturel pour avoir accès aux monuments.
example_3: Le pass culturel est rentable si vous prévoyez beaucoup de visites.
example_1_translation: The Paris Pass includes entry to more than 60 museums.
example_2_translation: I took the cultural pass to have access to the monuments.
example_3_translation: The cultural pass is profitable if you plan a lot of visits.

communicative_purpose: To express admiration for a castle or palace
variations: le palais est superbe, c'est impressionnant, le château est beauté
common_mistakes: 'Magnifique' is stronger than 'joli'; use for impressive structures
example_1: Le château de Versailles est magnifique, on y passe la journée.
example_2: Ce château Renaissance est vraiment magnifique avec ses jardins.
example_3: On a fait des photos everywhere, le château est magnifique le soir.
example_1_translation: The Palace of Versailles is magnificent, we spend the day there.
example_2_translation: This Renaissance castle is truly magnificent with its gardens.
example_3_translation: We took photos everywhere, the castle is magnificent in the evening.

communicative_purpose: To refer to taking photos or a photograph
variations: la photo, la photo de, prendre en photo, le selfie
common_mistakes: 'Photo' is short for 'photographie' - both used interchangeably
example_1: Je veux prendre une photo devant la Tour Eiffel.
example_2: On peut prendre des photos à l'intérieur du musée ?
example_3: La photo souvenir est obligatoire pour ce genre de voyage !
example_1_translation: I want to take a photo in front of the Eiffel Tower.
example_2_translation: Can we take photos inside the museum?
example_3_translation: Souvenir photos are mandatory for this type of trip!

communicative_purpose: To indicate that advance booking is required
variations: la réservation est nécessaire, obligatoire de réserver, book avant
common_mistakes: Many tourist sites require advance booking, especially in summer
example_1: Pour la tour Eiffel, il faut réserver à l'avance, c'est complet.
example_2: Il faut réserver au moins une semaine avant pour ce spectacle.
example_3: En haute saison, il faut réserver plusieurs jours en avance.
example_1_translation: For the Eiffel Tower, you must book in advance, it is full.
example_2_translation: You must book at least one week in advance for this show.
example_3_translation: In high season, you must book several days in advance.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the historic student and academic quarter of Paris
trigger_situations: Paris tourism, historical districts, academic areas
variations: le Quartier Latin, le 5ème arrondissement, le quartier étudiant
common_mistakes: Latin Quarter is the traditional English name; 'Quartier Latin' in French
example_1: Le Quartier Latin est connu pour ses librairies et ses cafés.
example_2: On s'est promené dans le Quartier Latin après la visite de Notre-Dame.
example_3: La Sorbonne est located dans le Quartier Latin.
example_1_translation: The Latin Quarter is known for its bookstores and cafes.
example_2_translation: We walked in the Latin Quarter after visiting Notre-Dame.
example_3_translation: The Sorbonne is located in the Latin Quarter.
